Dashboard Archive: 2026-03-25 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: Momentum Ranker Data Today's data reveals an extraordinary outlier in TNGX, which ranks #1 with a composite score of 97.2 based on explosive returns across all timeframes—most notably a 1,071.84% one-year return and a 1-month return of 58.31%, dramatically outpacing SPY (+62.82% over the same period). TNGX passes all technical gates (above its 30, 50, 100, and 200-day moving averages) and shows strong ratio consistency with 4 of 5 ratios passing thresholds, though its 0.3824 bad-SPY score indicates it has held up reasonably well during market downturns. In contrast, PL ranks second with a 767.25% one-year return but shows weaker ratio consistency (only 3 passes) and a notably higher bad-SPY score of 2.8074, suggesting it has been more vulnerable during periods of SPY weakness. Both tickers show broad negative correlation with defensive and materials sectors (XLP, XLB, XLI), indicating they operate in growth-oriented spaces disconnected from traditional market hedges. The data suggests a market environment where certain high-conviction growth names have significantly decoupled from broader indices.
